summary: Clean soundboard captures a high‑energy desert show with standout “Here Comes Sunshine”, “Uncle John’s Band” and “The Last Time”, despite a few rough moments.
review: The SBD recording is crisp and well‑balanced, letting Bobby's gritty guitar and the band’s desert‑vibe shine.  The performance is full of memorable moments – a surprise opener with Here Comes Sunshine, powerful renditions of Uncle John’s Band, The Last Time and China Doll, plus an intense Space segment – though some songs suffer from vocal slips and a few wonky transitions.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is ~4.3 stars; most comments praise the show and note a clean soundboard, with only occasional performance or technical glitches.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clean, crisp mix; Bobby’s guitar sits high in the front.  Minor issues: late mic cut‑ins, a few vocal rough spots and occasional transition dropouts, but overall very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The show opens with an unexpected, uplifting Here Comes Sunshine and builds through a tight first set.  The second set delivers heavy jams and emotional peaks (Uncle John’s, The Last Time, China Doll) before a solid encore, despite a few rough vocal moments and a couple of transition hiccups.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as soulful, weathered and surprisingly revitalized/younger; one reviewer notes an off‑night with very rough voice on a later song.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s (Weir) guitar is prominent and gritty throughout, praised especially on Althea and Throwing Stones; also noted for occasional lyrical slip‑ups and a generally ‘off’ night.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s keyboards get special praise on the “Greatest Story” segment and add texture to the jam sections; machine‑gin sounds highlighted as intriguing.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drummers (Bill) receive kudos for intense, fun Space/Last Time passages and overall strong rhythmic drive.
